notes: Bishop of Bourges in the 7th century, recipient of a letter from Desiderius of Cahors.

docs.title: Life of Caius Marius
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: Sulpicius, tribune of the people, a bold and confident man, contrary to everybody’s expectation, brought forward Marius, and proposed him as proconsul and general in that war.

docs.title: Life of Sulla
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: He was cruel, bold, rapacious, and in all these points utterly shameless and unscrupulous; not hesitating to offer Roman citizenship by public sale to freed slaves and aliens, and to count out the price on public money-tables in the forum.

docs.title: Letter 10
docs.author: Desiderius of Cahors
docs.subject_summary: To Bishop Sulpicius of Bourges: Ecclesiastical matters
docs.excerpt: To the most holy Lord Sulpicius, bishop of Bourges, from his brother Desiderius of Cahors, greetings in Christ, I am, I confess, somewhat in awe of your tenure at Bourges.
